name: Finnish Barley Porridge
steps: Simmer the barley in the milk for 30-35 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and tender.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the barley in the milk and cinnamon over low heat, stirring occasionally, 32-38 minutes, until thick and tender.
Top generously with the honey - ohrapuuro, barley porridge, is an old, plain breakfast still eaten across Finland, especially in the countryside.
description: A simple barley porridge, barley being a Finnish pantry staple.

name: Finnish Rye Bread with Cheese

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the rye bread and top with the cheese.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the rye bread generously, top with the cheese, and finish with a little black pepper.
Dense, dark rye bread is eaten at nearly every meal in Finland, plain or topped with cheese, cold cuts, or fish.
description: Rye bread, Finland's everyday bread, paired here simply with cheese.

name: Finnish Eggs with Dill
steps: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter.
Fold in the dill just before they finish setting.
steps_detailed: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter over low heat, stirring often for soft curds.
Fold in the dill and pepper just before they finish setting - dill is used as freely in Finland as in the rest of the Nordic countries, especially alongside egg and fish dishes.
description: A simple egg breakfast, dill being a defining Scandinavian herb.

name: Finnish Salmon Soup
steps: Simmer the potato in water until nearly tender, about 12 minutes.
Add the salmon and milk and simmer 8-10 more minutes until the fish is just cooked.
Stir in the dill before serving.
steps_detailed: Soften the onion in the butter, 5-6 minutes, then add the potato and 1 cup water and simmer 12-14 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the salmon and milk and simmer 8-10 more minutes until the fish flakes easily.
Stir in the dill just before serving - lohikeitto, creamy and simple, is one of the most beloved everyday dishes in Finland.
description: Lohikeitto, a creamy salmon soup, one of Finland's most beloved comfort foods.

name: Finnish Meatballs with Cranberry
steps: Mix the beef with the onion and form into small balls.
Pan-fry in the butter until browned through, 8-10 minutes.
Simmer the cranberries with a splash of water and a little sugar into a sauce, and serve alongside.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef with the onion and pepper, and form into small, even balls.
Pan-fry in the butter over medium heat, 9-11 minutes, turning, until browned through.
description: Simmer the cranberries with the sugar and a splash of water, 6-8 minutes, into a loose sauce, and serve alongside - lihapullat traditionally come with lingonberry, and cranberry fills much the same tart role here.

name: Finnish Rutabaga Casserole
steps: Simmer the rutabaga in water until very tender, 20-25 minutes, then mash.
Mix in the milk, egg, and half the butter, transfer to a small dish, dot with the remaining butter, and bake at 375F for 25-30 minutes.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rutabaga in water until very tender, 22-28 minutes, then drain and mash well.
Mix in the milk, egg, pepper, cinnamon, and half the butter, transfer to a small baking dish, dot with the remaining butter, and bake at 375F (190C) for 28-32 minutes until set and golden on top.
Lanttulaatikko is a fixture of the Finnish Christmas table, its faint sweetness pointed up by a touch of cinnamon.
description: Lanttulaatikko, a rutabaga casserole, a Finnish Christmas staple.

name: Finnish Cabbage Rolls
steps: Blanch the cabbage leaves briefly until pliable.
Mix the beef, rice, and onion, roll into the cabbage leaves, and bake at 375F, covered, for 35-40 minutes.
steps_detailed: Blanch the cabbage leaves in simmering water 2-3 minutes until pliable, then drain.

Mix the beef, rice, onion, and pepper, roll firmly into the cabbage leaves, arrange in a baking dish with the tomatoes poured over, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 38-42 minutes until the rice is fully cooked.
description: Kaalikaaryleet, sturdy and filling, is a genuine Finnish home-cooking staple, especially through the colder months.

name: Finnish Roast Lamb with Barley
steps: Season the lamb with the pepper and shape into a compact portion.
Roast at 375F for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
Simmer the barley with the onion and 1.5 cups water for 30-35 minutes until tender, and serve alongside.
steps_detailed: Season the lamb with the pepper and shape into a compact, even portion.
Roast at 375F (190C) for 22-28 minutes, turning once, until well browned and cooked through.
Simmer the barley with the onion, butter, and 1.75 cups water for 32-38 minutes until tender, and serve alongside.
description: Roast lamb with barley, barley being a Finnish pantry staple.

name: Finnish Game-Style Saute
steps: Brown the beef and onion in the butter until deeply cooked, 10-12 minutes.
Season generously with the pepper and serve.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef and onion in the butter over medium-high heat, 11-13 minutes, until deeply browned.
Add the milk and simmer 4-5 minutes into a light sauce, seasoning generously with the pepper.
description: Poronkaristys, thin-sliced sauteed reindeer in a milky pan sauce, is one of Finland's most iconic dishes and a Lapland specialty - reindeer isn't a distinct item here, so ground beef stands in.

name: Finnish Beef and Rutabaga Stew
steps: Brown the beef with the onion and pepper.
Add the rutabaga and 1.5 cups water, and simmer 25-28 minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, pepper, and butter over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the rutabaga and 1.75 cups water, and simmer 28-32 minutes until the rutabaga is fully tender.
A simple, hearty root-vegetable stew of exactly the kind that carries Finnish households through the coldest months.
description: A simple beef and rutabaga stew, rutabaga being a common Finnish root vegetable.

name: Finnish Baked Salmon with Dill
steps: Season the salmon with the dill and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until just cooked through. Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Season the salmon with the dill and pepper and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 16-20 minutes until just cooked through and flaking easily.
Finish with the lime juice - simply prepared salmon with dill turns up constantly across the Nordic countries, Finland included.
description: A simple baked salmon, dill being a defining Scandinavian herb.

name: Finnish Rye Crust Pastries

ingredients.ingredient: Bob's Red Mill Organic Dark Rye Flour

steps: Simmer the rice in the milk until thick.
Mix the rye flour into a thin dough, shape into small boats, fill with the porridge, and bake at 450F for 12-15 minutes.

ingredients_full.note: cooked into a soft porridge for the filling

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bob's Red Mill Organic Dark Rye Flour

ingredients_full.note: melted, plus more for the butter-egg topping
hard-boiled and mashed, for the traditional munavoi topping

steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk over low heat, stirring often, 25-28 minutes, until it breaks down into a thick, soft porridge; cool slightly.

Mix the rye flour with a little water and a pinch of the butter into a firm, thin dough - real rye flour, with barely any gluten, makes a dough that's much more fragile and short than wheat flour, and needs to be handled gently.
Roll the dough very thin and cut into small oval shapes, pinching up the edges to form a shallow boat shape.
Fill each with the rice porridge and bake at 450F (230C) for 12-15 minutes until the edges are browned and slightly crisp.
Mash the hard-boiled egg into the melted butter to make munavoi, and spread over the warm pastries before serving.
description: Karjalanpiirakka, rice porridge baked in a thin rye crust, is one of Finland's most iconic foods - real rye flour, with barely any gluten, makes a fragile, short dough that needs gentle handling, and the rye crust is the entire identity of the dish.

name: Finnish Turnip and Cheese Bake
steps: Layer the turnip with the milk in a small dish and bake at 375F, covered, for 20 minutes.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 10-12 more minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Layer the turnip with the milk, butter, and pepper in a small baking dish, cover, and bake at 375F (190C) for 20-22 minutes until nearly tender.
Uncover, top with the cheese, and bake 12-14 more minutes until golden and bubbling.
description: A simple turnip and cheese bake.

name: Finnish Rye and Herring Snack

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the rye bread and top with the pickled herring.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the rye bread generously, top with the pickled herring, and finish with a scattering of fresh dill.
description: Rye bread and herring, a common Finnish snack pairing.

name: Finnish Barley and Cranberry Salad
steps: Toss the cooked barley with the cranberries, lime juice, and oil.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
steps_detailed: Toss the cooked, cooled barley with the cranberries, lime juice, oil, and pepper.
Let sit 10-15 minutes so the flavors meld before serving, at room temperature or chilled.
description: Barley and cranberry, a common Finnish flavor pairing.

name: Finnish Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
Top with the cranberries.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top with the cranberries and a drizzle of honey - riisipuuro, with a cranberry or fruit sauce, is a Finnish Christmas Eve tradition, much like its Norwegian counterpart.
description: A simple rice pudding.

name: Finnish Honey Cake
steps: Cream the butter and honey, beat in the egg, then fold in the flour.
Bake at 350F for 20-22 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and honey together until light, then beat in the egg.
Fold in the flour, cinnamon, and baking powder, pour into a small lined pan, and bake at 350F (175C) for 22-26 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
description: A simple honey cake.

name: Finnish Cranberry and Barley Pudding
steps: Simmer the barley in the milk for 30-35 minutes until very tender and creamy.
Stir in the cranberries and honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the barley in the milk and cinnamon over low heat, stirring occasionally, 32-38 minutes, until very tender and creamy.
Stir in the cranberries and honey, letting the berries soften slightly in the residual heat.
description: A simple cranberry and barley pudding.

name: Finnish Dill and Butter Sauce
steps: Whisk the melted butter with the dill and lime juice.
Spoon over fish while still warm.
steps_detailed: Whisk the melted butter with the dill, lime juice, and pepper.
Spoon over fish or boiled potatoes while still warm.
description: A simple dill-butter sauce, dill being a defining Scandinavian herb.

name: Finnish Cranberry Sauce
steps: Simmer the cranberries with the sugar and a splash of water, 8-10 minutes, until it breaks down into a sauce.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cranberries with the sugar, cinnamon, and a splash of water over medium-low heat, 9-11 minutes, until it breaks down into a thick, glossy sauce.
Serve alongside meatballs or roasted meats - lingonberry is the traditional pairing, and cranberry fills the same tart, cutting role.
description: A simple cranberry sauce, a common Finnish accompaniment to meat dishes.
